data.title: Why a Ruby reader (and Rails compatibility)?
data.excerpt: Ruby still runs Shopify, GitHub, Airbnb, Stripe. Rails powers the long tail of business web apps. Here's why @webhouse/cms ships a first-class Ruby reader.
data.content:
## The Ruby audience

Ruby on Rails is the framework that brought MVC to the masses. Shopify, GitHub, Airbnb, Stripe, Zendesk, Basecamp — all built on Rails. The Ruby community is smaller than Python's but deeply committed to developer happiness.

## Sinatra vs Rails

This example uses **Sinatra** for fast bring-up — no `rails new`, no asset pipeline, no Active Record. Just a single `app.rb` file and a `webhouse.rb` reader. The whole thing boots in under a second.

But the reader works **identically** in Rails. Drop `webhouse.rb` into `app/lib/`, then in any controller:

```ruby
class BlogController < ApplicationController
  def index
    cms = Webhouse::Reader.new(Rails.root.join('content'))
    @posts = cms.collection('posts', locale: 'en')
  end
end
```

No other changes needed.

## What's in the package

- `Webhouse::Reader#collection(name, locale:)` — list published documents
- `Webhouse::Reader#document(collection, slug)` — load one document
- `Webhouse::Reader#find_translation(doc, collection)` — sibling via translationGroup
- `Webhouse::Reader#globals` — cached singleton site doc
- `Webhouse.string(doc, key, default)` — safe field accessor
- Path traversal protection via `SAFE_NAME` regex + expand_path prefix check

~100 lines of Ruby. Stdlib only.
data.author: Christian Broberg
